title: Social Security
description: Practice understanding the workings of Social Security and how it affects retirement planning.

assessment_items.question_content:
Social Security plays a crucial role in the financial well-being of millions of Americans.

**What type of benefits does Social Security provide?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Social Security plays a crucial role in the financial well-being of millions of Americans.

**Which of the following is a common misunderstanding about Social Security?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It provides benefits to people with disabilities.
It is a type of insurance program, not a retirement savings account.
It is a type of retirement account, like an IRA or 401(k).
It provides benefits to retired people.

assessment_items.question_content:
Social Security is also known as the OASDI program.

**What does the acronym "OASDI" stand for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Old Age, Savings, and Disability Insurance
Old Age, Survivors, and Disability Insurance
Old Age, Security, and Disability Insurance

assessment_items.question_content:
Social Security provides financial benefits for retirees, survivors, and individuals with disabilities.

**Who funds Social Security?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

While you are working you pay payroll taxes that contribute to Social Security.

**What happens to this money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It goes into a personal account for your future use.
It goes to a retirement account for you and your employer.
It pays benefits to people who are currently retired or disabled.
It funds other government programs, like roads and parks.

assessment_items.question_content:
At time of retirement, you can begin claiming Social Security benefits.

**Who will be funding your Social Security payments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: future workers still in the workforce

assessment_items.question_content:
Social Security is at risk of running out of funds because more people are retiring and fewer workers are paying into it. 

**How may the government address this problem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Social Security is at risk of running out of funds because more people are retiring and fewer workers are paying into it. 

**How may the government address this problem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Social Security is at risk of running out of funds because more people are retiring and fewer workers are paying into it. 

**How may the government address this problem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Let's assume you are currently working and paying into Social Security. At one point, the government may decide to raise the retirement age to address the shrinking of the Social Security fund. 

**How will this decision affect your finances and retirement planning?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You may need to work longer before receiving full benefits.
You can retire earlier with no change in benefits.
Your monthly Social Security payments will increase.
You will stop paying Social Security taxes sooner.

assessment_items.question_content:
Let's assume you are currently working and paying into Social Security. At one point, the government may decide to raise payroll taxes to address the shrinking of the Social Security fund. 

**How will this decision affect your finances and retirement planning?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You will take home less money from your paycheck.
You will receive higher Social Security benefits.
You will stop paying taxes sooner.

assessment_items.question_content:
Let's assume you are currently working and paying into Social Security. At one point, the government may decide to lower the [[☃ definition 1]] to address the shrinking of the Social Security fund. 

**How will this decision affect your finances and retirement planning?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: monthly payment you receive after you retire

choices.content: You may need to save more on your own for retirement.
You will receive larger monthly Social Security payments.
You will be able to retire earlier with the same benefits.
You will stop paying into Social Security earlier.
